data_basis: NORA10 hindcast (waves/wind) 1958-2009 + on-site current measurements; NORSOK N-003 extremes
operability_relevance: Seasonal/monthly + directional scatter supports W2W/SOV gangway and CTV access persistence analysis
directional_dist_provided: yes - 12 x 30deg sectors for wind, wave, current (Tables 2.11, 3.9, 4.9)
surface_current_omni_50yr_cms: estimated:read 0-deg dominant sector ~136-139 cm/s; full omni row not isolated in extraction - see gap
directional_to_omni_uplift: +~10% on directional extremes at 10^-2 annual prob per NORSOK procedure
monthly_dist_provided: yes - seasonal/monthly tables for operability windows (Tables 2.12, 3.10)

data_basis: API 2INT-MET interim hurricane criteria (synthetic storm / hindcast)
operability_relevance: Hurricane-season extremes; operability windows for PSV/AHTS dominated by tropical-cyclone avoidance, not these design extremes
applicability: Waves listed for WD >= 1000 m; currents WD >= 150 m; wind all depths; north of 26N
region: US Gulf of Mexico - Western (97.5W to 95.0W)

applicability: Waves WD >= 1000 m; currents WD >= 150 m; wind all depths; north of 26N
region: US Gulf of Mexico - West-Central (94.0W to 90.5W)

design_standard_context: Project metocean studies aligned to ISO 19901-1 / API RP 2MET regional approach
Hs_100yr_m_Forcados: 3.02 (estimated/secondary - Forcados offshore S Nigeria; from WASP study summary, primary table not verified)
regime: Persistent low-Hs long-period swell from S/SW (South Atlantic 40-60S storms) + secondary N Atlantic swell; benign winds, weak tropical-cyclone influence
operability_relevance: Low Hs but long-period swell drives vessel/gangway roll & heave resonance - operability often period-limited not height-limited; favourable for high CTV/W2W uptime if motion-tuned
region: West Africa - Gulf of Guinea (Nigeria, Cabinda, Angola)
mean_wave_period_s: approx 7 (associated with Forcados Hs100); swell Tp commonly 12-16 s

current_regime: Brazil Current + meso-scale eddies; strong surface/sub-surface currents (design values project-specific)
regime: Energetic SW swell from extra-tropical cyclones/anticyclones (winter/autumn dominant) + cold-front events; strong Brazil Current
operability_relevance: SW swell + strong current affects DP station-keeping (AHTS/PSV) and W2W headings; current is a primary operability driver alongside Hs
region: SE Brazil - Campos / northern Santos Basin

regime: Extreme sea states dominated by tropical cyclones; ~4.5 storms/season, avg ~2 reach Category 5; benign non-cyclone background
design_methodology: Synthetic tropical-cyclone databases to 100/1000/10000-yr levels; joint extreme current-with-depth profiles
Hs_100yr_m: gap (cyclone-driven; site-specific synthetic-storm databases used - no single public value verified)
operability_relevance: Long benign windows punctuated by severe cyclones requiring vessel sail-away; operability planning is cyclone-track / forecast-driven rather than fixed-Hs-limited

wind_profile: Logarithmic/power-law wind profiles, gust factors, NPD/Frya and Harris/Kaimal wind spectra (consistent with the dimensional 1-hr-mean profile formulas reproduced in API 2INT-MET Sec 4.1)
limit_state_use: ULS/ALS extreme return-period values and FLS scatter diagrams; operability uses short-term/seasonal statistics
provides: Short-term (per sea-state) statistical models; long-term joint metocean distributions; single-parameter and joint Hs-Tp distributions for design load conditions across limit states
scatter_diagram_role: Defines wave scatter diagram concept (Hs vs Tp/Tz cell occurrence) and joint long-term models used to build region-specific scatter diagrams for fatigue & operability/persistence analysis
region: DNV-RP-C205 - methodology layer for environmental conditions & loads (all regions)
current: Current profile models (tidal + wind-driven + density-driven), depth variation
relationship_to_regional_records: C205 is the methodology; regional numbers (North Sea, GoM, etc.) are produced by applying C205-type models to local hindcast/measurement data
scope: Rational design criteria & guidance for wind, wave, current and tide loads on marine structures incl. ships, fixed/floating offshore units, mobile units, marine renewables
